intro: Dapsilanthus ramosus is a plant species in the family Asphodelaceae, commonly known as the "Dancing Lady" due to its unique, drooping inflorescences. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, with its health benefits attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Dapsilanthus ramosus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Dapsilanthus ramosus is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which are commonly used to treat respiratory infections and fever.
